title: Boat Rental in Hardangerfjord: How to Choose the Right Boat in 2026
html:
Hardangerfjord, Norway's second-longest fjord, offers a stunning backdrop for a boating holiday. Renting a boat from Eidfjord or Utne lets you explore waterfalls and glaciers, with prices from EUR 350 for a half-day motorboat. Browse options on BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace for boat rentals.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Hardangerfjord?

Prices range from EUR 250 for a small motorboat for two hours to EUR 2,500 for a crewed yacht for a full day. A half-day small motorboat typically costs EUR 350 to EUR 500. For a catamaran full-day charter, budget EUR 800 to EUR 1,200 per group. Shared tours, such as a fjord sightseeing trip from Odda, cost about EUR 100 per person. Always book a charter online in advance for the best availability.

Where to set off in Hardangerfjord?

Several real harbours and marinas serve as ideal departure points. Eidfjord Harbour is a popular base near Vøringsfossen waterfall. Utne Marina offers sheltered berths and easy access to the Folgefonna glacier. The port of Odda provides a gateway to the famous Trolltunga hike. Other options include Rosendal Marina, with views of Folgefonna, and Lofthus, a quiet village pier perfect for kayak launches. Each location has its own character and proximity to key attractions.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests), Ideal for a half-day exploring the inner fjord arms; from EUR 350.
  2. Bowrider or day cruiser (6-8 guests), Suitable for families; from EUR 600 for a full day.
  3. Catamaran (8-12 guests), Offers stability for longer voyages; from EUR 900 full-day.
  4. Motor yacht (e.g. Princess F70, 8-10 guests), For luxury cruising with crew; from EUR 1,800 day. See this motor yacht for details.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Hardangerfjord?

High season runs from June to August, when the weather is mild and the days are long. In July, expect the local wind known as "fjordbris" to pick up in the afternoon, so plan morning departures for calm conditions.

June through August are the peak months. May and September also offer good conditions, with fewer crowds. Always check the weather forecast before setting out.

Regarding licences: you generally need a valid driving licence for motorboats up to 25 feet, but the operator will confirm exact requirements. Larger vessels may require a boat licence or ICC. Note that Norway has specific rules for non-EU residents, so verify with your rental company.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 250 to EUR 2,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to August
  • Trip length: Half-day (4 hours) to full-day (8+ hours)
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests typical
  • Currency: Norwegian Krone (NOK) commonly used, but many operators quote in EUR

Questions about boat rental in Hardangerfjord

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Hardangerfjord?

For small motorboats up to 25 feet, a valid driving licence is usually sufficient; larger boats need a boat licence or ICC, but always confirm with the operator.

Can I rent a boat without prior boating experience?

Yes, many operators offer a short briefing and will provide a simple safety demo, and you can also hire a skipper for an additional fee.

What happens if the weather turns bad?

Rental companies typically have a cancellation policy for unsafe conditions; some offer a rain check or a refund if the forecast is severe.

Is it possible to fish from a rented boat?

Yes, but you need a valid fishing licence for saltwater fishing, which can be purchased online or at local shops.

Whether you are planning a half-day cruise to the Folgefonna glacier or a week-long expedition to the outer fjord, renting a boat in Hardangerfjord is a rewarding adventure. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Hardangerfjord or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Hardangerfjord and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
